title: Crunchy Venezuelan Bake
description: A resinous and crunchy ovo-vegetarian-friendly dish inspired by Venezuelan cuisine, featuring vegan white wine vinegar.

ingredients.name: Crushed plantain chips
ingredients.notes: available at Latin American markets or online.

instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit (190 degrees Celsius).
In a large bowl, mix together the cassava flour, coconut oil, vegan white wine vinegar, salt, and black pepper until well combined.
Add the grated Parmesan cheese, chopped cilantro, crushed plantain chips, egg, and warm water to the bowl. Mix until a dough forms.
Knead the dough on a floured surface for 5 minutes, until smooth and elastic.
Divide the dough into 8 equal pieces.
Shape each piece into a ball and then flatten slightly into a disk shape.
Place the disks on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper, leaving about 1 inch of space between each disk.
Brush the tops of the disks with a little extra coconut oil.
Bake for 15-20 minutes, or until the tops are golden brown and the edges are crispy.
Remove from the oven and let cool for 5 minutes before serving.
Dust with a little extra grated Parmesan cheese, if desired.
Serve warm, garnished with chopped cilantro if desired.
Store leftovers in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 3 days.
